source_pr.title: ddl, ci: force domain to reload schema before execute SQL.
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: #19481 #19480 #19479 

Problem Summary:

DDL test may fail on high CPU load. After do some test, we found that golang's timer will generate a huge bias on high CPU load. So we need to force call `Domain.Reload()` manually.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

Add `Domain.Reload()` function call on failed cases.

### Related changes

- No changes

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test
- Integration test

Side effects

- No

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- ddl: make CI test more stable.

symptoms: PR body: DDL test may fail on high CPU load due to Golang timer bias; force call Domain.Reload() manually
failure_signature: PR body: DDL test may fail on high CPU load due to Golang timer bias; force call Domain.Reload() manually
root_cause_explanation: Under high CPU load, schema updates can be observed late/stale in tests (e.g., due to timer/lease timing bias). These DDL tests executed schema-sensitive SQL/checks while the in-memory InfoSchema might not be refreshed yet. This PR stabilizes the tests by forcing `Domain.Reload()` before executing SQL and before checking table locks, ensuring the session sees an up-to-date schema version.
fix_pattern: Force `Domain.Reload()` in schema-sensitive DDL tests before executing SQL/checks to avoid stale InfoSchema under load.

evidence: evidence: PR body: DDL test may fail on high CPU load due to Golang timer bias; force call Domain.Reload() manually
evidence: add `forceReloadDomain` helper and call `dom.Reload()` before executing SQL / checking table lock in tests (ddl/db_change_test.go, ddl/db_test.go)
